description_long: This object is a pale, cream-colored crab with a broad, slightly convex carapace and two prominent, asymmetrical claws extending forward. The crab's body is segmented into a cephalothorax and a tucked abdomen, with the ventral side showing the characteristic plates and joints. It has eight slender walking legs, each jointed and splayed outward, giving the crab a wide stance. The claws are robust, with one noticeably larger than the other, and the tips are slightly darker. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, flanked by small, visible antennae. The surface of the crab is hard and slightly textured, typical of a crustacean exoskeleton.
description: A pale crab with a broad carapace, two large claws, eight splayed legs, and short eye stalks. Its hard, textured shell and segmented body are clearly visible.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's broad carapace, both claws extended, and all eight legs spread outward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the crab's body depth, the curvature of the claws, and the layering of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmented underside of the crab, with the legs and claws partially visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the crab's lateral structure and the arrangement of its appendages.
